original: 500 ton meteorite lands near Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
Marlene Raymond (15), limboes under a flaming bar at 6 1/8"
Rookie outfielderJoe DiMaggioties 3 MLB records in New York's 10-run 5th inning against the White Sox, hitting 2 home runs for 8 total bases; Yankees beat Chicago, 18-4
Jan Kubiš, Czech paratrooper in WWII (killed Nazi head of Bohemia Reinhard Heydrich in Operation Anthropoid), born in Dolní Vilémovice, Moravia, Austria-Hungary (d. 1942)
First demonstration of a home video recorder at BBC Studios in London
Future Baseball Hall of Fame infielderJackie Robinsonsteals home for first of 19 times in his career as the Brooklyn Dodgers beat Pittsburgh Pirates, 4-2
CardsVince Colemansteals record 39th & 40th consecutive bases
Battle of Bannockburn: Scotland regains independence from England
Flying saucers sighted over Mount Rainier by pilot Ken Arnold
Outfielder Jack Reed hits his only MLB home run in the 22nd-inning as NY Yankees edge Detroit Tigers, 9-7 in slowest extra-inning game in league history; 7:00 hours
1st French: spationaute Jean-Loup Chrétien and 2 Soviet cosmonauts, lift off aboard Soyuz T-6 from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan, USSR
First exhibition byPablo Picasso, aged 19, opens in Paris
NBA Draft: LSU centerShaquille O'Nealfirst pick by Orlando Magic
Sudden outbreak of St. John's Dance causes people in the streets of Aachen, Germany, to experience hallucinations and begin to jump and twitch uncontrollably until they collapse from exhaustion
Joan Clarke, English cryptanalyst and code-breaker at Bletchley Park during World War II (Enigma Project), born in London (d. 1996)[1]
Lowell Cross, American electronic music composer, instrument maker and laser light show inventor, born in Kingsville, Texas
New York Giants outfielder Wes Westrum hits 3 HRs & a triple in a 12-2 rout of the Cincinnati Reds at the Polo Grounds V
Vikings sail up the Loire with 67 ships and destroy Frankish-held city of Nantes
First detection of an airplane using reflected radio waves, a precursor to radar, by US Naval Research Laboratory engineers in Anacostia, Washington, D.C.
Moon tremor perceived (hit by Taurid meteors)
Stanley Cup Final, Meadowlands Arena, East Rutherford, NJ: New Jersey Devils beat Detroit Red Wings, 5-2 for a 4-0 series sweep; Devils' first Stanley Cup finals appearance
Roy O. Disney, American businessman and co-founder of the Walt Disney Company with brother Walt, born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1971)
Ellison Onizuka, American Air Force Major and astronaut (STS 51C, 51L - Challenger disaster), born in Kealakekua, Hawaii (d. 1986)
10th recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
David Rittenhouse observes a total solar eclipse in Philadelphia
Former slave Mary Prince is the first women to partition the UK parliament - to return to the West Indies as a free person
First performance of "O Canada," the song that would become the national anthem of Canada, at the Congrès national des Canadiens-Français
The Moscow Victory Parade takes place
11.72" (29.77 cm) of rainfall at Mellen, Wisconsin (state 24-hr record)
India is dismissed for 42 in 17 overs in 2nd Test defeat to England at Lord's; lowest total in country's Test cricket history; Chris Old 5-21, Geoff Arnold 4-19
7th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Challenger 2 lands at Edwards AFB
USAF reports Roswell 'space aliens' were dummies
NBA Draft: Southwest Atlanta Christian Academy power forward Dwight Howard first pick by Orlando Magic
James IVis crowned King of Scotland at Scone Abbey in Scone, aged 15
3rd Rugby World Cup, Ellis Park, Johannesburg: Springboks fly-half Joel Stransky lands the winning drop goal in extra time as South Africa defeats New Zealand 15-12
Fred Hoyle, English astronomer (proposed steady-state universe theory, coined phrase 'Big Bang'), born in Gilstead, England (d. 2001)
Martin Lewis Perl, American physicist (1995 Nobel Prize for Physics for discovery of the tau lepton particle), born in New York City (d. 2014)
Arthur Brown, British rock singer and songwriter (The Crazy World of Arthur Brown - "Fire"), born in Whitby, Yorkshire
Derrick "Duckie" Simpson, Jamaican rocker (Black Uhuru), born in Kingston, Jamaica
Natalya Shaposhnikova, Soviet artistic gymnast (Olympic gold 1980), born in Rostov-on-Don, USSR
Period of relative peace following WWII exceeds that following WWI
18th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (51-G): Discovery 5 returns to Earth
Challenger moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 51-F
NHL adopts instant-replay and tenth of second clock in final minute

Christie’s Auction House in New York City conducts sale of 80 ofEric Claptonand friends' guitars to benefit Crossroads Centre at Antigua, a drug and alcohol residential rehabilitation facility he co-founded in 1997, raising over $5M; "Blackie", a 1956-57 composite Fender Stratocaster used as his primary instrument form the mid 70s until 1985 sells for a then record $959,500
Joe Frazierstops Mexican challenger Manuel Ramos in 2nd round TKO at NYC's Madison Square Garden in his first heavyweight boxing title defense
Crystal Gaylereleases best-selling album "We Must Believe in Magic" featuring worldwide hit "Don't It Make My Brown Eyes Blue" (1st platinum album by a female country music artist)

Christie’s Auction House in New York City conducts sale of 100 ofEric Clapton’s guitars to benefit Crossroads Centre at Antigua, a drug and alcohol residential rehabilitation facility he co-founded in 1997, raising nearly $4.5M; "Brownie", a 1956 Fender Stratocaster used on the original recording of "Layla" sells for a then record $450,000
"Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest", directed by Gore Verbinski, starringJohnny Depp,Keira KnightleyandOrlando Bloom, premieres at Disneyland - becomes fastest film to gross over 1 billion
Agrippina J. Vaganova, Russian ballet dancer, and pedagogue (Fundamentals of the Classical Dance), born in Saint Petersburg, Russian Empire (d. 1951)
Harry Partch, American composer (Revelations in the Courthouse Park; Delusion Of The Fury), and instrument inventor, born in Oakland, California (d. 1974)[1]
Jean Deslauriers, Canadian violin prodigy, conductor (CBC, 1935-78), and composer, born in Montreal, Quebec (d. 1978)
Pearl Witherington, British WW II secret agent for the Special Operations Executive, born in Paris, France (d. 2008)
Betty Stöve, Dutch professional tennis player (6 Grand Slam Doubles, 1972-79; 4 Grand Slam Mixed Doubles, 1977-81), and coach, born in Rotterdam, Netherlands
Raelene Boyle, Australian athlete (Olympic silver 200m 1968, 72; 100m 1972), born in Melbourne, Australia
Juli Inkster, American golfer (7 LPGA major titles; US Open 1999, 2002), born in Santa Cruz, California
Curt Smith, British-American rock bassist and vocalist (Tears For Fears - "Everybody Wants To Rule The World"; "Shout"), born in Bath, Somerset, England

Uwe Krupp, NHL ice hockey defenseman, 1986-2003 (2 x All- Star; 2 x Stanley Cup; Buffalo Sabres, Colorado Avalanche, and 3 other teams; Olympics. 1998 (Germany)), and European coach, born in Cologne, Germany
Maya official Ajpach’ Waal pays an important diplomatic visit to Waxaklajuun Ub’aah K’awiil (18 Rabbit), 13th king of Copán (modern Honduras)
Sultan Bajezid I releases captured crusaders for ransom
